stance: Risk-on momentum-led manufacturing book: concentrate where price strength is confirmed by rising analyst revisions; cut momentum-dead and revision-falling legacy names.
experience_note: This is an early round with no graded track record yet (last_round_review is null), so I have nothing to learn from prior calls and must lean on signal discipline, not hindsight. What I am watching to judge whether this thesis is working: (1) Do the high-momentum + rising-revision leaders (MTAR, GE Shipping, National Aluminium, Atlanta Electricals, CP Plus) actually out-deliver the names I exited over the next 1-3 months - if the names I dropped beat my adds, my "confirmed momentum" filter added no value. (2) Does MTAR's extreme momentum (a 3-4 sigma price move) mean-revert hard - I sized it below the per-name cap precisely because parabolic momentum is fragile; a sharp reversal there would tell me I over-trusted a single outlier. (3) Do the revision-falling names I exited (Adani Power, Sona, ABB, Praj) keep falling - if instead they rally, my reliance on analyst-revision direction as a confirmation filter was misplaced. (4) Whether my heavy Industrials weight (~40% of equity) helps or hurts relative to NIFTY 500 - if the manufacturing/capex theme cools, this concentration is my biggest active risk. I am pre-registering that I will NOT re-chase any name I exited this round just because it bounces; I cut them on a stated rule (momentum rolled over, or revisions falling), and a single bounce does not refute the rule.
